metadata.notes: Handles multiple extension scenarios for resource access management, including policies for conversion, compatibility, and selection, as well as handling holding timers and exceptions.

inputs.vague: A service client requests access to a resource, uses it, and then signals that it is finished. The resource lock then cleans up allowing other clients to potentially use the resource.
inputs.detailed:
UC CC1: Serialize access to a resource
Main Success Scenario
1) Service Client asks a Resource Lock to give it specified access.
2) The Resource Lock returns control to the Service Client so that it may use the Resource.
3) Service Client uses the Resource.
4) Service Client informs the Resource Lock that it is finished with the Resource.
5) Resource Lock cleans up after the Service Client.
Extensions
2a. Resource Lock finds that Service Client already has access to the resource.
2a1. Resource Lock applies a conversion policy (see CC 4) to the request.
2b. Resource Lock finds that the resource is already in use:
2b1. The Resource Lock applies a compatibility policy (see CC 2) to grant access to the
Service Client.
2c. Resource Locking Holding time limit is non-zero:
2c1. Resource Lock starts the holding timer.
3a. Holding Timer expires before the Client informs the Resource Lock that it is finished:
3a1. Resource Lock sends an Exception to the Client’s process.
3a2. Fail!
4a. Resource Lock finds non-zero lock count on Service Client:
4a1. Resource Lock decrements the reference count of the request.
4a2. Success!
5a. Resource Lock finds that the resource is currently not in use:
5a1. Resource Lock applies a selection policy (see CC 3) to grant access to any
suspended Service Clients.
5b. Holding Timer is still running:
5b1. Resource Lock cancels Holding Timer.

groundTruth.name: Serialize access to a resource
groundTruth.summary: This use case describes how a Service Client acquires and releases access to a shared resource managed by a Resource Lock, including various policies for handling concurrent access and timeouts.

steps.description: Asks a Resource Lock to give it specified access.
Returns control to the Service Client so that it may use the Resource.
Informs the Resource Lock that it is finished with the Resource.
Cleans up after the Service Client.
Applies a conversion policy to the request.
Applies a compatibility policy to grant access to the Service Client.
Sends an Exception to the Client’s process.
Decrements the reference count of the request.
Applies a selection policy to grant access to any suspended Service Clients.
